patches.summary: Typed child-table upserts fall back to parent_id for generated parent foreign keys.
patches.reason: The generated dependent sync path injects parent_id, but several typed child tables require resource-specific parent columns such as transactions_id.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes the generated UpsertBatch dependent parent-id tests.

patches.summary: README documents local app startup, source OpenAPI URL, and the exact reprint command.
patches.reason: The generated README is publish-oriented; this CLI is currently consumed from the local generated tree while distribution is undecided.
patches.validated_outcome: README includes install, auth, and local development instructions.

patches.summary: CLI auth and config prefer THECLOSE_BASE_URL and THECLOSE_API_TOKEN.
patches.reason: The Close agents need stable project-owned env vars rather than generated provider-shaped names.
patches.validated_outcome: auth status and config get report THECLOSE_API_TOKEN without printing token values.

patches.summary: Classified CLI errors use canonical The Close auth hints and redact bearer tokens.
patches.reason: Agent output must be machine-actionable and safe to log without leaking API tokens.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli covers compact projection and token redaction.

patches.summary: Adds high-level deals, fields, and task workflow commands on top of generated endpoint wrappers.
patches.reason: Agents need transaction-coordination verbs and a one-command deal workspace without bypassing The Close API.
patches.validated_outcome: deals/tasks/fields help and dry-run mutation commands work from the built CLI.

patches.summary: Adds high-level actions lifecycle commands for proposal, dry-run, status, approval, execution, runs, and audit.
patches.reason: Connector-backed provider work must be controlled by The Close rather than direct agent writes to downstream systems.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... and built CLI help/dry-run checks cover action idempotency, approval-boundary output, and proposal audit helpers.

patches.summary: Adds FUB helper commands that create The Close connector action proposals using catalog capability IDs.
patches.reason: Agents need convenient Follow Up Boss workflows without bypassing The Close's proposal, approval, dry-run, and audit controls.
patches.validated_outcome: FUB helper dry-runs show proposal payloads with follow_up_boss.actions capability IDs and generated idempotency keys.

patches.summary: Updates MCP/skill packaging for workflow commands, canonical auth, and no-bypass provider guidance.
patches.reason: IDE/chat agents need the same The Close control-plane boundary as shell users, with MCP bundle auth and skill examples aligned to the high-level commands.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./..., go build ./..., printing-press verify-skill, and printing-press bundle all pass.

patches.summary: Documents mutating command risk classes, tests action idempotency bodies, and redacts provider secret-shaped fields from dry-run/error output.
patches.reason: Connector-adjacent agent workflows need safe previews, idempotency replay behavior, TC approval boundaries, and publishable docs without local paths or sample PII.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./..., go build ./..., printing-press verify-skill, printing-press pii-audit --strict, and The Close bun test/build pass.

patches.summary: Expands local sync/search coverage and adds work-queue commands for overdue, blocked, approval, closing, missing-field, and stale-action queues.
patches.reason: Agents need local-first repeated reads and compound cross-deal questions without fan-out API calls, while The Close API remains authoritative.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./..., go build ./..., built CLI help checks, printing-press verify-skill, printing-press tools-audit, and printing-press pii-audit --strict pass.

patches.summary: Captures the tc-wghg.11 dogfood transcript and lets local work-queue approval checks surface synced connector proposal events.
patches.reason: The API exposes connector proposal status by ID but not a list endpoint, so local approval queues need the synced activity-event signal until a list route exists.
patches.validated_outcome: The generated CLI completed the redacted local dogfood loop; go test ./..., go build ./..., printing-press dogfood, scorecard, verify-skill, and pii-audit --strict pass.

patches.summary: Documents initial Printing Press library distribution, versioning, release artifacts, and reprint maintenance gates.
patches.reason: The Close CLI needs an explicit install and maintenance story while staying outside the app repo until long-term ownership is decided.
patches.validated_outcome: README includes local go install, post-publish Printing Press install, API-version-linked release line, MCP bundle command, and release verification commands.
